name: Bundle size optimization (React/Next.js)
description: CRITICAL: reduce initial bundle size and import overhead. Includes: avoid barrel imports, conditional module loading, defer third-party, dynamic imports, analyzable import paths, preload based on intent.

metadata.intent: Reduce bundle size and import costs for faster TTI/LCP and dev builds

must_not: Accidentally import huge libraries through barrels when deep imports/optimizer exists
conventions: If Next.js supports import optimization, prefer it over unsafe deep imports without types.

steps.description: Avoid barrel imports that re-export thousands of modules (icons, MUI). Prefer Next.js import optimization or safe direct imports.
Load big modules/data only when feature is enabled (and gate SSR bundles with `typeof window !== 'undefined'` when appropriate).
Defer analytics/logging/error tracking until after hydration (e.g. `next/dynamic` with `ssr: false`).
Use `next/dynamic` to lazy-load heavy UI (Monaco, charts) not needed for initial render.
Prefer statically analyzable import/fs paths. Use explicit maps like `{ key: () => import('...') }` rather than `import(map[key])`.
Preload code based on user intent (hover/focus prefetch) to reduce perceived latency without bloating initial bundle.
